techniques.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
techniques.evidence_quote: a dependency in our container base image contained malware (a Monero miner)

During a pilot with a fintech customer, our cluster became victim to a crypto-jacking attack... a dependency in our container base image contained malware (a Monero miner) which triggered inside the customer's environment
our cluster became victim to a crypto-jacking attack... racked up a massive cloud bill as a tiny startup

attack_chain.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_chain.description: A threat actor embedded a Monero cryptominer as malware within a dependency pulled in by the victim's container base image. This poisoned dependency was unknowingly included in the ML ops toolkit being built on Kubernetes.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a dependency in our container base image contained malware (a Monero miner)

attack_chain.description: The victim team deployed their containerized ML ops toolkit on a Kubernetes cluster during a fintech customer pilot. This caused the malicious container image (containing the poisoned dependency) to be instantiated and executed in the customer's environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: During a pilot with a fintech customer, our cluster became victim to a crypto-jacking attack... a dependency in our container base image contained malware (a Monero miner) which triggered inside the customer's environment

attack_chain.description: Once the malicious container executed, the embedded Monero miner activated and consumed cloud compute resources for cryptocurrency mining, resulting in a massive cloud bill for the startup.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: our cluster became victim to a crypto-jacking attack... racked up a massive cloud bill as a tiny startup

initial_access_vector: Malicious Monero miner embedded in a dependency of a container base image used in the victim's ML ops toolkit, introduced via software supply chain compromise
